title: hemigenia incana
intro: Hemigenia incana is a shrub native to Australia, belonging to the mint family Lamiaceae. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues. The plant has been found to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it beneficial for soothing skin irritations and wounds, as well as providing relief from pain and fever. Hemigenia incana can be prepared as a tea or infused oil, or used in topical applications to harness its medicinal benefits.
